title: Quiz: Motional Modes
description: Test your understanding of ion chain normal modes

questions.question: For two ions in a linear trap, what is the stretch mode frequency relative to the axial trap frequency omega_z?
questions.explanation: The stretch mode, where ions move in opposite directions, has frequency sqrt(3)*omega_z due to the combined restoring force from the trap and the Coulomb repulsion.

questions.question: What is the Lamb-Dicke parameter eta?

options.text: The ratio of phonon number to qubit frequency
The ratio of ion displacement to laser wavelength
The coupling strength between internal and motional states
The trap depth in temperature units

questions.explanation: The Lamb-Dicke parameter eta = k*sqrt(hbar/2m*omega) characterizes the coupling strength between the ion's internal state and its motion. It determines sideband transition strengths.

questions.question: The center-of-mass (COM) mode has the highest frequency among the axial modes.
questions.explanation: False! The COM mode has the lowest frequency (equal to the single-ion trap frequency). Higher modes have progressively higher frequencies due to the additional restoring force from Coulomb interactions.

questions.question: Why does anomalous heating in ion traps limit gate fidelity?

options.text: It adds phonons during the gate, disrupting the spin-motion coupling
It causes the trap to become unstable

questions.explanation: Anomalous heating adds thermal phonons to the motional mode during the gate. Since entangling gates rely on precise spin-motion coupling, extra phonons introduce errors and reduce fidelity.
